source.author: SSA Catalog
source.notes: Foundational vinegar-based shawarma marinade base (concentrate) to scale and adapt for beef/lamb/chicken/veg.

ingredients.label: Red wine vinegar
notes: Sub: apple cider vinegar for a fruitier tang.
For a cleaner tang: distilled white vinegar (reduce slightly; it’s sharper).

ingredients.label: Olive oil
notes: This is a concentrate; you can add more oil when adapting for specific proteins.

ingredients.label: Onion powder
notes: About 1 tablespoon. Sub: 120 g grated onion (use immediately; shorter storage).

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: This is a base concentrate; salt can be adjusted per application.

ingredients.label: Brown sugar (or honey)
notes: Optional: balances acidity and helps browning (about 1 tablespoon).

steps.title: Mix the base concentrate
instructions: In a bowl or jar, combine red wine vinegar, lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, and onion powder. Whisk or shake until emulsified.
Add all spices, black pepper, salt, optional sugar/honey, and optional chili flakes.
Whisk until uniform. If storing in a jar, shake vigorously until spices are dispersed.

steps.title: How to use (scaling notes)
instructions: This is a concentrate base designed to be adapted per protein/veg and batch size.
Typical usage: 60–120 ml base per 900 g–1.4 kg (2–3 lb) protein or vegetables.
For lean meats (chicken breast), consider adding 30–60 ml yogurt or 15–30 ml extra oil to mellow acidity.
For beef/lamb, you can use the base as-is or add 15–30 ml oil for richer coating.

steps.title: Marinate time guidance
instructions: Chicken: 2–12 hours (best 6–10). Avoid much beyond 24 hours due to acidity.

steps.title: Storage
instructions: Store base in a sealed jar in the refrigerator.
For best flavor, use within 7–10 days.
If you substituted fresh grated onion, use within 2–3 days.

steps.title: Food safety
instructions: If the base touches raw meat, do not reuse it unless you boil it thoroughly.
Always marinate proteins in the refrigerator.
